summary: A minimal, clean implementation of the GPT architecture for training causal language models. It demonstrates the decoder-only Transformer pattern, from tokenization and embedding to self-attention and iterative generation.

concepts.name: GPTConfig
concepts.subtitle: Hyperparameter definition
concepts.description: Centralizes all model dimensions and settings like n_layer and n_embd. This ensures consistency across the model's layers and allows for easy experimentation with different model scales.
concepts.ask: Why is it better to use a config class rather than passing arguments to every layer?

concepts.name: Data Pipeline
concepts.subtitle: Text-to-tensor preprocessing
concepts.description: Handles the conversion of raw text into integer sequences and batches them using memory-mapped files for efficiency. It creates the shifted target sequences (y = x[1:]) necessary for causal language modeling.
concepts.ask: How does the data pipeline prepare the targets for causal prediction?

concepts.name: Transformer Block
concepts.subtitle: The core computational unit
concepts.description: Implements a single layer of the Transformer consisting of Causal Self-Attention and a Feed-Forward Network (MLP). It uses residual connections and LayerNorm to stabilize training and allow for deep architectures.
concepts.ask: What is the purpose of the residual connections in the Block forward pass?

concepts.name: GPT Model
concepts.subtitle: The full model assembly
concepts.description: Orchestrates the token and position embeddings, the stack of Transformer blocks, and the final linear head. It transforms a sequence of token IDs into a probability distribution over the vocabulary.
concepts.ask: How are token embeddings and position embeddings combined in the GPT model?

concepts.name: Forward Pass & Generation
concepts.subtitle: Inference and prediction logic
concepts.description: The forward pass computes logits for the next token, while the generate method implements an autoregressive loop. It feeds the model's own predictions back as input to create new text.
concepts.ask: Explain how the generate method handles sequences that exceed the block_size.

concepts.name: Training & Optimization
concepts.subtitle: Learning and weight updates
concepts.description: Manages the optimization process, including a cosine learning rate schedule with warmup. It distinguishes between parameters that require weight decay (2D tensors) and those that don't (biases/norms).
concepts.ask: Why does the optimizer treat 2D parameters differently from 1D parameters?
